phases.name: Walking Skeleton: Static Header Auth End-to-End

steps.name: Schema migration: auth_mode and static_headers fields
steps.description: Add auth_mode enum, static_headers array, oauth_account, and discovery_source fields to mcp_server and credential_provider tables. Backfill existing servers with provider references to auth_mode=provider.
steps.criteria: POST /mcp-servers with auth_mode=static_headers and a static_headers array creates an mcp_server record. The record has auth_mode set to 'static_headers' and the response includes the server id.
steps.scenario_name: creates an mcp_server with auth_mode static_headers via API

steps.name: Encrypt static header values at rest
steps.description: When creating an MCP server with static_headers, encrypt each header value using the existing AES-256-GCM encryption module before persisting to SurrealDB. Store as value_encrypted, never plaintext.
steps.criteria: Reading the mcp_server record directly from SurrealDB shows static_headers[*].value_encrypted containing ciphertext that does not match or contain the original plaintext value.
steps.scenario_name: stores header values encrypted, not as plaintext

steps.name: Mask header values in API responses
steps.description: GET /mcp-servers returns has_static_headers=true and header names only. Header values (plaintext or encrypted) are never included in any API response body.
steps.criteria: GET /mcp-servers response body does not contain any header secret values. Response includes has_static_headers=true for servers with static headers configured.
steps.scenario_name: GET response includes header names but not values

steps.name: Inject static headers on MCP client connection
steps.description: Wire the credential resolver to decrypt static headers and inject them into the MCP client transport when connecting to a server with auth_mode=static_headers. Complete the walking skeleton end-to-end path.
steps.criteria: When Brain connects to an MCP server configured with static headers, the HTTP request to the MCP server includes the configured Authorization header. tools/list returns tools from the authenticated server.
steps.scenario_name: MCP client sends configured headers when connecting to server

steps.name: Update static headers on existing server
steps.description: PUT /mcp-servers/:id/headers replaces all static headers with new encrypted values. Old values are removed from storage.
steps.criteria: After PUT with new headers, subsequent MCP connections use the updated header values. The old header values are no longer stored in the database.
steps.scenario_name: replaces existing headers with new values

steps.name: Remove all static headers when switching auth mode
steps.description: When admin updates auth_mode from static_headers to none, the static_headers field is cleared from the database record.
steps.criteria: After updating auth_mode to 'none', the mcp_server record in SurrealDB has static_headers cleared (NONE or empty).
steps.scenario_name: switching auth_mode to none clears stored headers

steps.name: Reject restricted header names
steps.description: Validate header names against a blocklist (Host, Content-Length, Transfer-Encoding, Connection). Return 400 with a validation error if any restricted name is used.
steps.criteria: POST /mcp-servers with static_headers containing name 'Host' returns 400 with a validation error message.
steps.scenario_name: rejects Host, Content-Length, Transfer-Encoding, Connection

steps.name: Multiple headers on same server
steps.description: Support creating and injecting multiple static headers (e.g. Authorization + X-Custom-Header) on a single MCP server.
steps.criteria: When an MCP server is configured with two static headers, both headers are present in the HTTP request when Brain connects to the MCP server.
steps.scenario_name: stores and injects multiple key-value pairs

steps.name: Discover auth from Protected Resource Metadata (RFC 9728)
steps.description: POST /mcp-servers/:id/discover-auth fetches /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource from the MCP server URL, then fetches the authorization server metadata from the discovered auth server URL.
steps.criteria: Given a mock MCP server serving Protected Resource Metadata, discover-auth returns discovered=true with the auth server URL, authorization_endpoint, and scopes.
steps.scenario_name: fetches .well-known/oauth-protected-resource and auth server metadata

steps.name: Discover auth from WWW-Authenticate header fallback
steps.description: When /.well-known/oauth-protected-resource is not available, fall back to making a request that triggers a 401 and parse the resource_metadata URL from the WWW-Authenticate header.
steps.criteria: Given an MCP server without .well-known metadata but returning 401 with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer resource_metadata=URL, discovery succeeds by following the resource_metadata URL.
steps.scenario_name: falls back to WWW-Authenticate when .well-known is not available

steps.name: Auth server metadata multi-endpoint fallback
steps.description: When the auth server URL has a path component, try RFC 8414 path-based, OIDC path-based, and OIDC suffix-based well-known endpoints in order.
steps.criteria: Given auth server URL with path component (e.g. /tenant1), discovery tries the three fallback URLs in order and uses the first successful response.
steps.scenario_name: tries multi-endpoint fallback for auth servers with path components

steps.name: Discovery fails gracefully when no metadata available
steps.description: When the MCP server does not serve Protected Resource Metadata and does not return WWW-Authenticate, discover-auth returns discovered=false with an error message.
steps.criteria: Given an MCP server with no discovery endpoints, discover-auth returns { discovered: false, error: '...' } with a descriptive error.
steps.scenario_name: returns discovered=false when no metadata is available

steps.name: Auto-create credential_provider from discovery
steps.description: On successful OAuth discovery, automatically create a credential_provider record with the discovered authorization_url, token_url, and discovery_source set to the MCP server URL.
steps.criteria: After successful discovery, a credential_provider record exists with discovery_source matching the MCP server URL and authorization/token endpoints matching the discovered values.
steps.scenario_name: creates a credential_provider with discovery_source set

steps.name: Generate authorization URL with PKCE S256
steps.description: POST /mcp-servers/:id/authorize generates a PKCE code_challenge (S256), state parameter, and returns the full authorization URL with resource parameter (RFC 8707).
steps.criteria: Response includes redirect_url containing the authorization_endpoint, code_challenge_method=S256, a state parameter, a resource parameter matching the MCP server URI, and a scope parameter.
steps.scenario_name: returns authorization URL with code_challenge and state

steps.name: Exchange authorization code for tokens
steps.description: OAuth callback receives the authorization code and state, looks up the stored code_verifier, and exchanges the code at the token endpoint using PKCE.
steps.criteria: Given a valid authorization code and matching state, Brain exchanges the code at the token endpoint with the code_verifier and receives access_token + refresh_token.
steps.scenario_name: callback exchanges code using PKCE code_verifier

steps.name: Encrypt and store tokens in connected_account
steps.description: After successful token exchange, encrypt access_token and refresh_token using AES-256-GCM and store in a connected_account record linked to the mcp_server.
steps.criteria: Reading connected_account from SurrealDB shows access_token_encrypted and refresh_token_encrypted containing ciphertext (not plaintext). token_expires_at is set.
steps.scenario_name: access_token and refresh_token are encrypted in connected_account

steps.name: Token refresh on expiry
steps.description: When the credential resolver detects an expired access_token with a valid refresh_token, automatically call the token endpoint with grant_type=refresh_token before returning headers.
steps.criteria: Given an MCP server with expired access_token but valid refresh_token, the credential resolver refreshes the token, stores the new encrypted token, and returns headers with the new Bearer token.
steps.scenario_name: automatically refreshes expired tokens before MCP connect

steps.name: Refresh failure surfaces auth_error status
steps.description: When token refresh fails (expired refresh_token or server error), set the MCP server last_status to auth_error so the admin can re-authorize.
steps.criteria: Given an MCP server with both expired access_token and expired refresh_token, after a failed refresh attempt the MCP server last_status is 'auth_error' and auth-status endpoint returns 'expired'.
steps.scenario_name: sets last_status to auth_error when refresh fails

steps.name: Dynamic client registration
steps.description: When the discovered auth server provides a registration_endpoint, Brain registers itself as an OAuth client by posting client metadata. Received client_id and client_secret are encrypted and stored.
steps.criteria: Given a mock auth server with registration_endpoint, Brain POSTs client metadata (client_name, redirect_uris, grant_types) and stores the received client_id and encrypted client_secret.
steps.scenario_name: registers Brain as OAuth client when registration_endpoint exists

steps.name: No-auth server resolves to empty headers
steps.description: resolveAuthForMcpServer returns an empty headers map when the MCP server has auth_mode=none.
steps.criteria: Given MCP server with auth_mode=none, resolveAuthForMcpServer returns an empty object ({}).
steps.scenario_name: resolveAuthForMcpServer returns {} for auth_mode none

steps.name: Static headers server resolves to decrypted headers
steps.description: resolveAuthForMcpServer decrypts AES-256-GCM encrypted header values and returns them as a plain header map for auth_mode=static_headers.
steps.criteria: Given MCP server with auth_mode=static_headers and encrypted header values, resolveAuthForMcpServer returns the decrypted header map (e.g. { Authorization: 'Bearer ghp_test123' }).
steps.scenario_name: resolveAuthForMcpServer decrypts and returns stored headers

steps.name: OAuth server resolves to Bearer token
steps.description: resolveAuthForMcpServer decrypts the access_token from the linked connected_account and returns it as an Authorization: Bearer header for auth_mode=oauth.
steps.criteria: Given MCP server with auth_mode=oauth and a linked connected_account with valid access_token_encrypted, resolveAuthForMcpServer returns { Authorization: 'Bearer <decrypted_token>' }.
steps.scenario_name: resolveAuthForMcpServer returns Authorization: Bearer from connected_account

steps.name: Provider server resolves via existing credential flow
steps.description: resolveAuthForMcpServer delegates to the existing credential_provider resolution logic for auth_mode=provider, maintaining backward compatibility.
steps.criteria: Given MCP server with auth_mode=provider and a linked credential_provider + connected_account, resolveAuthForMcpServer returns headers matching the existing credential resolution behavior.
steps.scenario_name: resolveAuthForMcpServer delegates to existing credential_provider logic
